text.title: Business Process Analysis — Enterprise Scale and Governance Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real engagements applying business-process-analysis. Each entry pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Lifting APQC PCF text verbatim as if it describes the actual company processes.
antipattern.root-cause: Reference-model fundamentalism — author copy-pastes APQC text into the current-state model.
antipattern.fix: Reference processes are industry averages; every reference-derived sentence must be tagged [reference] and reviewed before publication.

antipattern.symptom: Documenting the ideal process instead of the actual process.
antipattern.root-cause: Sponsor and BA describe the policy intent because nobody shadowed an operator.
antipattern.fix: Current-state model must reflect observed reality; pair with frontline-validation-protocol two-operator rule.

antipattern.root-cause: Stakeholders pressure the BA to move to future-state design before measurement.
antipattern.fix: Lock volume/cycle-time/cost baselines before any future-state work; Stage 4 is gated on baseline presence.

antipattern.symptom: Documenting 800 processes uniformly without a Pareto cut.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent over-eagerly produces equal-depth artefacts for every inventory row.
antipattern.fix: Apply r7: deep-model only top decile by nva_minutes_per_year × strategic_fit; narrative-document the rest.

antipattern.symptom: M&A surviving entity's process documented as canonical even when target's is better.
antipattern.fix: Require ownership_decision citation back to deal-thesis line; un-cited decisions are blocked.
